qt: While solving a LP by Simplex method, which of the following corresponds to corner points of the feasible region in Graphical method?

The variants of the simplex method cannot be identified through numerical examples.

After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables, then in a given iteration, how many Basic feasible VARIABLES are possible to exists?
Which method is also known as the Charnes method of penalty in the artificial variable technique?
While solving a Linear Programming problem by Simplex Method, an inequality says:‘2x-3y>5’ is transformed into strict equality of the form---------, where ‘s>=0’

How can a minimization problem can be converted into maximization problem?
op: By multiplying each coefficient in the objective function by-1

qt: The inequality $3x - 2y \geq 14$ is equivalent to______.

If the point (t,6) lies in the feasible region associated with constraint:2x+3y<=12, then the maximum value of ‘t’ would be----------.

To remove artificial variable(s) from the objective function, we have  __________ method(s).
In the Simplex method to solve an LP problem of maximization, if at the end of an iteration, there is a negative coefficient in the objective row then the given problem---------.

$${\text{Minimizing}}\,\,{\text{any}}\,\,{\text{function}}\,f({x_1},{x_2},{x_3},......,{x_n})\,{\text{subject}}\,{\text{to}}\,{\text{the}}\,{\text{set}}\,{\text{of}}\,{\text{constraints}}\,\,{\text{is}}\,\,{\text{equivalent}}\,{\text{to}}\,{\text{maximizing _________ subject}}\,{\text{to}}\,{\text{the}}\,{\text{same}}\,{\text{set}}\,{\text{of}}\,{\text{constraints}}{\text{.}}$$
While applying Simplex method to a LP of maximization type, we proceed stepwise from one basic feasible solution to another in such a way that the objective function always ---------- its value.
While solving a LP problem by Simplex method in a given iteration, the new basic variable is ---------- and the variable remove from the basis is called ---------.
If initial basic solution is -----------, while solving an LP problem then no further iteration can be performed.
In an LP problem, to evaluate the basic variables in Simplex method, which of the following method is used?
Shortcoming of Big M method is that the value of M  could be ____________.
While applying Simplex method to a LP of minimization type, we proceed stepwise from one -------------solution to another in such a way that the objective function always increases its value.
For $Z = 4 x_1 + x_2$ subject to $3 x_1 + 4 x_2 \geq 20, x_1 + 5 x_2 \geq 15$, we have ___________ variables.
Big M technique is used if the initial basic solution obtained using slack variables is _______.

After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables. If the initial basic feasible solution is  say;(2,4,1,2), then it is ----------  solution.
While solving an LP problem by Simplex method, the inclusion of slacks in the constraints’ inequalities helps in finding ------------- variables.
In a feasible region, if the end points of the line segment are basic solutions, then all the points between these two will ------------.
Convex set is the collection of all -------------.
While solving a Linear programming problem, we find -------------number of basic feasible solution.
An optimal solution of a Linear Programming problem is necessarily a Basic feasible solution.
Which of the following is true about the inclusion of non-negative slack variable into a constraint of type ‘less than or equal’?
op: Inequality is transformed into type: ‘greater than or equal’
Inequality is transformed into strict equality constraint
This inclusion doest not affect the inequality
Inequality become redundant and hence skips from the LP problem.

qt: By Simplex method, to minimize ‘Z = 9x–2y’of an LP problem, if ‘z=A>0’ for the initial iteration then for its next improved solution(0<A<100), which of the following would be the next entering variable?

By Simplex method, to minimize ‘Z = 2x+9y’of an LP problem, if ‘z=A>0’ for the initial iteration then for its next improved solution(0<A<100), which of the following would be the next entering variable?
After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables. Then how many Combinations of Basic feasible Solutions may possible?
A Linear Programming problem may not have any feasible solution.
Which of the following order pair would minimize the objective function of the linear programming problem; $z = x + 5 y$ subject to $x \geq 2 , y \geq 0$ ?
If the optimum solution of a Linear Programming problem is finitely unique and then its corresponding objective function must have------------ maximum or minimum.
If any or all of the artificial variables do not leave the basis in the final solution, then this indicates that the problem _________ .
In the Simplex method to solve an LP problem of maximization, if at the end of iteration, every entry of objective function row is positive then the given problem------------.

After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables. If the initial basic feasible solution is say;(2,4,1,2), then its corresponding non-basic solution is---------.
In two phase method process, first phase ____________ the sum of artificial variables.
op: maximize or minimize depending on the situation

qt: Simplex method can only be applied to problems with two decision variables.
In Simplex standard table to solve an LP problem of Maximization, we choose the candidate for entering variable in-------.
op: Objective function with most positive coefficient
Objective function with most negative coefficient

qt: In the Simplex method, if all the elements in columns of the ratio by taking the RHS of each row and dividing by the corresponding element of the key column, are equal, the situation is known as ----------

For a LP problem say;Max:z=x+y,under the constraints x,y>=0, the feasible region would be--------.
The role of artificial variables in the 'Big M' technique is to _______________.
op: ensure feasibility of the initial basic solution

qt: In two phase method if the minimum value of objective function in the first phase is greater than zero, then the solution of original problem _____________.
While applying Simplex method to a LP of minimization type, we proceed stepwise from one basic feasible solution to another in such a way that the objective function always ---------- its value.
By which of the following method, any complex linear programming problem can be handled?
The solution set of the constraint: 2x+3y>12, will lie in --------- bisected by the line:2x+3y=12.
op: opposite to the origin side in half plane

qt: While solving a Linear Programming problem by Simplex Method, an inequality constraint is transformed into strict equality by the inclusion of ---------- variable.
The Simplex method is a-------------- for solving Linear Programming problem.
While solving a Linear Programming problem by Simplex method, we convert all the constraints'inequalities into------------.
op: nothing but keep those strictly the same

qt: In linear programming, cycling occurs when the algorithm repeatedly follows the same sequence of iterations without converging to an optimal solution.
In Simplex method to solve an LP problem, Gauss Jordan Elimination method demands that all the key column entries should be zero except-----.
op: row with maximum ratio on RHS column

qt: By Simplex method, to maximize ‘Z = 2x +9y’of an LP problem, if ‘z=0’ for the initial iteration then for its next improved solution, which of the following would be the next entering variable?
By Simplex method, to maximize ‘Z = 2x–9y’of an LP problem, if ‘z=0’ for the initial iteration then for its next improved solution, which of the following would be the next entering variable?
Solution region of the constraints: ‘2x+3y>12’ and ‘2x+3y<12’ will be the---------.
The solution set of the constraint: 2x+3y<12, will lie in --------- bisected by the line:2x+3y=12.
op: opposite to the origin side in half plane

qt: When we have $\geq$ type constraints, then we convert it to equality constraints by introducing _______ variable(s) and solve it by __________ method.
op: artificial and slack, Big M or Two phase

qt: After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables, then in a given iteration, how many Infeasible VARIABLES are possible to exists?
When ___________ fail to provide an initial basic feasible solution in the simplex method, we use the Big M Method.
In Simplex standard table to solve an LP problem of Minimization, we choose the candidate for entering variable in-----------.
op: Objective function with most negative coefficient
Objective function with most positive coefficient

qt: Once an LP problem is converted to a table, then calculation steps would be same for _____________ method(s).
While solving a Linear Programming problem by Simplex Method, an inequality says:‘2x-3y<5’ is transformed into strict equality of the form---------, where ‘s>=0’.
The cost coefficient of artificial variable in Objective function is -------------.
For $Z = 4 x_1 + x_2$ subject to $3 x_1 + 4 x_2 \geq 20, x_1 + 5 x_ 2 \geq 15$, we have ___________ variables.

After converting constraints into the respective Standard equalities, we have an LP problem of ‘4’ equations in ‘6’ variables, if the initial basic feasible solution is say;(2,4,0,2), then it is ----------  solution.
In an LP problem, to evaluate the basic variables in Simplex method in any iteration, we would have to express Objective function in terms of ------------- variables.
The purpose of phase 1 in the two phase method is to ________ .
op: to minimize the sum of artificial variables

qt: While solving an LP problem by Simplex method, if we have ‘3’ constraint equations in ‘5’ variables then to find basic variables we have to put any ------- variables equal to zero.

In graphical solution of solving Linear Programming problem to convert inequalities into
equations, we ----------------.
op: simply assume them to be equations

qt: The straight line associated with the constraint;2x+3y<12,will meet x-axis at a distance of-------- units from origin.

The positive slack variables indicate -----------------.
If in a LP problem, the objective is to maximize: z = 2x+3y, with all associated constraints of ‘=<’ type, then in the 1st iteration, z = ---------.
For a LP say;‘Min:z = x+y’ under the constraints x,y>=0, the optimal solution would be--------.
In the Two Phase Method the objective of Phase 2 is to _____________.
op: find the optimum basic solution for the original problem

qt: In the Simplex method to solve an LP problem of minimization, if at the end of iteration, there is a positive coefficient in the objective row then the given problem -------------.
The first step in the 'Big M' technique is ________________.
op: introducing artificial variables for constraints involving “≥”
expressing the linear programming problem in equation form

qt: When using Big M method, the objective function should not contain __________ variable(s).
In the Simplex method to solve an LP problem, Gauss Jordan Elimination method demands that all the key column's entries should be --------- except key row(pivot) entry.
When constraints are of greater than or equal to type, then we ______________.
op: subtract slack variable and add artificial variable
add slack variable and subtract artificial varialbe

qt: Which of the following will be annual optimum cost of a product if the number of annual orders WITHOUT any shortage is 25 and the total cost of one cycle is 10,000?

When we have to manage a project with available resources, we have the option of resource leveling. Here the resources availability is less than the maximum resources required for an activity, then it is feasible to divert the resources to -------------.
Which of the fiollowing is an intermediate step to model a linear programming problem?
op: Identify the non-negative restrictions and constraints

qt: In the ABC analysis, the items are classified into three categories with respect their--------------.
In Wilson’s model, Total cost = ------------
op: Item cost + holding cost + shortage cost + set up cost
Item cost + holding cost + shortage cost
Item cost + set up cost + holding cost
Holding cost + shortage cost + set up cost

qt: Which of the following would be the objective of the yield per minute in a chemical process which depends upon the temperature ‘x’ and pressure ‘y’?
Which of the following can NOT be considered to include in shortage cost?
op: Loss of goodwill of customers due to delay

qt: If the economic order quantity and the annual demand of a product are 2500 and 5000 units respectively, then WITHOUT any shortage the number of orders are ----------.
Which of the following technique to solve the network flow diagrams, is event oriented?
Which of the following cost for an item is independent of the quantity ordered or produced?
If the decision variables in primal problem are nonnegative then decision variables in dual are negative.
In the NWC method, which cell is chosen for allocation initially?
Which method is expected to give a better initial solution among NWC, LCM, and VAM?
op: All methods provide similar initial solutions

qt: In the LCM, what is done if the minimum cost is not unique?
op: Repeat the process with a new table

qt: A balanced transportation model with ‘5’number of  sources and ‘7’ destinations has -------- number of constraint equations.
If a variable in the Primal is unrestricted in sign, then the corresponding constraint in the dual will be of -------- type and vice versa.
Due to which of the following reason, Simplex method is not preferred to solve a Transportation problem?
op: Since Transportation problem does not contain positive slacks
Since it contains large number of decision variables ‘xij’s’ so that it becomes complicated
Since Transportation problem contains constraints of ‘=’ type
Since Transportation problem does not contain negative slacks

qt: In North West Corner method, the first step after choosing the appropriate cell in 1st row, we allocate -------------so that the capacity of first row or first column is exhausted.
The number of variables in the Primal will be the number of -------- in Dual and vice versa.
The Transportation and Transshipment Models are types of ---------------- models.
What is Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM) based on?
op: Considering only the maximum feasible amount

qt: The objective of a transportation problem is to develop an ----------- transportation schedule that meets all demand from given stock at a ------- total shipping cost.
In the Vogel’s approximation Method for solving a Transportation problem, Penalty measure for any row or column, is given by which of the following?
op: Difference between the smallest unit cost to the next smallest cost in the same row(column)
Difference between the largest unit cost to the next largest cost in the same row(column)
Difference between the Largest unit cost to the smallest unit cost in the came row(column)
Difference between any two arbitrary costs in the came row(column)

qt: In North West Corner method, in the first row and first column, available resource and sink contain ‘5’  and ‘7’ units respectively; then which of the following amount ‘x11’ will be allocated in the cell (1,1)?
If an LP problem contains large number of constraints and a smaller number of variables then which of the following will reduce the computational burden in finding its solution?
Under which of the following condition in the North West Corner Method, after first allocation in cell (1,1), next allocation along the diagonal in cell (2,2) can be made?
op: If in the first row and first column, each resource and sink contain equal units
If Total Supply is not equal to Total Demand

qt: The amounts shipped from a dummy source represent shortages at the receiving destinations
Primal of a dual is-----.
Primal of a Primal is-------.
What is the significance of selecting the row or column with the largest penalty in VAM?
op: It prioritizes the allocation to minimize penalties
It denotes the final allocation step
It represents the maximum supply or demand
It indicates the highest transportation cost

qt: The Transportation Problem is one of the sub-class of -------------.
In a Transportation problem, which of the following are taken as the coefficients of unknown “$x_{ij}$'s” ?
Dual of a Dual of Dual is------.
What is done with the row/column after an allocation is made in the LCM?
op: Adjust the demand at the destination
Retain the row/column for future allocations

qt: In transportation models, what does the decision variable xij typically represent?
op: The total demand at warehouse j.
The cost of transporting one unit from factory i to warehouse j.
The quantity of goods transported from factory i to warehouse j.
The total production capacity at factory i.

qt: For an unbalanced Transportation problem, if the total demand is LESS than total supply then which of the following is true in order to balance the problem?
op: A dummy source would have to include with supply equal to shortage
A dummy sink would have to include with demand equal to the surplus
One constraint will have to add

qt: For North West Corner method, in the first row and first column, each source and sink contain ‘5’ units ; then after allocating the appropriate amount ‘x11’ in the cell (1,1), we will move towards which of the following cell?
op: Towards (1,2) if 1st row contains more occupied cells than 2nd row
Neither towards (1,2) nor (2,1) but move diagonally towards (2,2)
Towards (2,1) if 2nd row contains more occupied cells than 1st row

qt: Which of the following Method is comparatively little complex to solve a Transportation problem?
What is the purpose of Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM) in solving transportation problems?
op: To ignore unit costs of transportation
To find a promising initial basic feasible solution

qt: For North West Corner method, in the first row and first column, resource and sink contain ‘5’ and ‘7’ units respectively; then after allocating the appropriate amount ‘x11’ in the cell (1,1), we will move towards which of the following cell?
For an unbalanced Transportation problem, if the total demand is MORE than total supply then which of the following is true in order to balance the problem?
op: A dummy sink would have to include with demand equal to the surplus
One constraint will have to add
A dummy source would have to include with supply equal to shortage

qt: Why do we use specialized algorithms for transportation problems instead of the simplex method?
op: The simplex method cannot handle equality constraints.
The simplex method is used for maximization only.

qt: In the Least Cost Method, for any cell both demand and supply are satisfied then which of the following will be crossed out?
Which of the following method to find the solution of a Transportation Problem can be observed as improved version of Least Cost Method?
If Primal Problem is a maximization problem, then the dual will be -------
If the primal has an unbounded solution, then the dual has------
For a Transportation problem, after finding the initial basic feasible solution, which of the following method is used to find the optimum solution?
If the total demand is equal to total supply as per requirement of a balanced transportation problem i-e “$a_1+a_2+ \cdots +a_n = b_1+b_2+\cdots+b_n$” then which of the following is true?
op: “$a_1=b_1, a_2 =b_2,\cdots, a_n=b_n$” is NOT necessarily implied

qt: In the Least Cost Method, allocations are made in a cell by assigning -----------to cell with smallest unit cost.
Which of the following method is used to find the initial basic feasible solution of a transportation problem?
Which of the following reason to prefer Vogel’s approximation method over other two methods?
op: Vogel’s method gives the feasible solution near to the optimal
Vogel’s method gives optimal time plan of transportation
Vogel’s method gives the shortest transportation rout
Vogel’s method allocate minimum amount to the variable with least unit cost

qt: How is the least cost method (LCM) different from the Northwest Corner rule (NWC)?
op: LCM starts from the bottom right corner
LCM allocates the maximum feasible amount
LCM follows a random allocation strategy
LCM considers unit costs of transportation

qt: How is Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM) different from the least cost method (LCM)?
op: VAM considers penalties for wrong allocations
VAM starts from the bottom right corner
VAM does not consider unit costs

qt: What is the penalty in Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM)?
op: The number of iterations required to find the optimal solution
The difference between the smallest and next to the smallest element in a row or column
The amount allocated to each cell in the transportation table

qt: For a Transportation Problem, if it’s initial feasible solution is evaluated by Least Cost Method, then quality of this initial solution is better than ----------.
After identifying the row with the least cost, what action is taken in the LCM?
op: Repeat the process for all columns

qt: What action is taken if a row or column is empty in the LCM?
In North West Corner Rule, we start initially from which of the following cell in the standard transportation table?
op: From any arbitrary cell in first row

qt: If Dual has a finite optimal solution, then the primal will------.
Which of the following can be said the main objective of the Least Cost Method to solve a transportation problem?
op: Concentrating on the routes with maximum available transportation time
Concentrating on the routes with cheapest cost of transportation
Concentrating on the routes with least transportation time
Concentrating on the routes with expensive cost of transportation

qt: Which step involves eliminating fully allocated rows or columns in the least cost method (LCM)?
The transportation problem can be represented mathematically as a linear programming model with the objective function of __________.
In a Transportation Problem, the objective function ’Z’ gives ----------.
op: Total inventory be supplied in transportation

qt: You realize that a row's total supply cannot fully satisfy the demand in any column. This means that_________.
op: You can ignore this row and continue.
The original transportation problem has no feasible solution.
You must add transportation costs to increase supply.
You have made an error in earlier allocations.

qt: The North – West Corner Rule
op: Is based on the concept of minimizing opportunity cost.
Is used to find an initial feasible solution
Is used to find optimal solution

qt: What does the difference between the smallest and next to the smallest element in a row or column represent in VAM?

For North West Corner method, in the first row and first column, each resource and sink contain ‘5’ units; then after allocating the appropriate amount ‘x11’ in the cell (1,1), in which of the following cell the next allocation will be zero?
op: Neither in (1,2) nor in (2,1) but in along the diagonal in (2,2)
Arbitrarily chosen either in (1,2) or in (2,1)

qt: How is the maximum feasible quantity determined in the LCM?
op: By selecting the next least cost
By multiplying the demand and supply

qt: The solution of a transportation problem with m rows (supplies) and n (destinations) is feasible if numbers of positive allocations are
Which values of “$a_{ij}$'s” in the standard form of a linear programming problem will transform it in standard Transportation problem?
Under which of the following condition in the Least Cost Method, after first allocation in cell (1,1), next allocation along the diagonal in cell (2,2) can be made?
op: If after crossed out 1st row or 1st column, cell (2,2) is of minimum cost
If in the second row and second column, each resource and sink contain equal units
If Total Supply is not equal to Total Demand

qt: The amount of the inventory will always taken to be -----------
In a Linear Programming problem which of following condition is compulsory?
op: Both Objective function and constraints need to be linear
Neither objective nor constraints to be linear
Least Objective function needs to be linear
Least constraints need to be linear

qt: While estimating the time for a PERT activity, the estimator will give the three times estimates. When these three estimates are plotted on a graph, the probability distribution that we get is closely associated with -----------------Distribution curve.
Which of the following is NOT an assumption of the basic Wilson's EOQ model?
op: Ordering costs are fixed per order.
There is a cost associated with shortages.

qt: Which of the following times are directly related to the activity cost that is by reducing the activity duration, the direct cost of the corresponding activity is increased?

Which of the following would be the objective of the cost per unit of producing certain cameras?

Which of the following Probabilistic time in PERT has the same analogical meaning of Deterministic time (time to complete any activity) in CPM?
Which of the following is an operations research (OR) process?
In a network flow diagram, if two jobs ‘a(i,j)’ and ‘b(i,k)’ of ‘4’ and ‘3’ days duration, finish late simultaneously on 7th day, then on which of the following day the event ‘i’ will finish earlier?
In a development project, if an activity (i,j) of six days duration, starts late on 3rd day then which of the following will be its latest finish time?
Which of the following situations would typically favor using PERT over CPM?
op: Construction project with a fixed budget

qt: Duration of dummy activity is _______.

In the phase of Early Start and Early Finish, to find the Critical Path in a network flow diagram, the computations are proceeded from --------------- to the final event.
Which of the following would be the objective of the daily loss of heat in a heating system?
Which of the following is the major objective of the ABC analysis of inventory?
op: To replace the required items in proper time
To classify the items in different categories
To fulfill the demand of items

qt: When resource limits conflict with a fixed project deadline, what is the likely outcome?
Under which of the following condition the economic order quantity in case of Purchasing Model with Shortage can be transformed into  Purchasing Model with no Shortage?
op: When Shortag Cost tends to infinity
When Carrying Cost tends to zero
When Carrying Cost tends to infinity
When shortage Cost tends to zero

qt: In an optimization problem, we seek for maximizing or minimizing a specific quantity, called the ----------.
Which of the following cost of an item vary directly with its lack of quantity and delaying time ?
In Wilson's model, what is the average inventory level?
op: Half of the maximum order quantity (Q/2)
Zero, since there are no shortages

qt: In the graph of  Purchasing Model with shortages, the area above  the horizontal axis represents -----------.
For an industrialist, optimization means ------------.
op: both maximizing its profit and minimizing its cost
change the business strategy and looking forward new customers

qt: Which of the following violates a fundamental rule of network diagram construction?

While finding the Critical Path, in the phase of Early Start and Early Finish computations, we start from -------- event and proceed calculations from left to right up to the final event.
CPM is abbreviation of ______________ .
In a development project, if an activity (m,n) of seven days duration, starts late on 3rd day then which of the following will be its latest finish time?
PERT is an acronym for ___________ .
op: Performance Evaluation and Review Technique Observation

qt: By putting the replacement rate ‘R?infinity’ in the Economic Order Quantity(EOQ) of Manufacturing Model with allowed Shortage, then the result is -----------.
op: EOQ of Manufacturing Model without Shortage
EOQ of Purchasing Model with allowed Shortage
EOQ of Purchasing Model without Shortage

qt: What is a common constraint in projects that require resource allocation?
A shortage of 20 units incurs a cost of Rs. 100/unit/day. If the shortage lasts for 5 days, what is the total shortage cost?
If the cost function in terms of economic order quantity is C = Q^2-400Q, then the minimum cost of inventory per unit of time is ---------- units.
Which is the following reason is essential for an enterprise to have an inventory?
op: It provides adequate service to the customers.
It reduces the possibility of duplication of orders.
It helps in smooth and efficient running of the business.

qt: The objective function and ----------- together form a Linear Programming problem.
Which of the following is uncertain in PERT?
In a development project, if an activity (m,n) of seven days duration, starts late on 5rd day then which of the following will be its latest finish time?

In Project Management, Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT) is based on ------------- times.

op: Late Start of the final event
Early Start of the final event

qt: Which of the following value is correct for the Variance of an activity times having optimistic, pessimistic and most likely values as 4, 8 and 6 days respectively?
Which of the following is true about the Manufacturing model without Shortage?
Linear Programming is the backbone of Operations Research models due to---------.
op: linearity of objective function and constraints

qt: Excess of inventories are undesirable due to the following reasons;
op: It may not lead to high volume of output instead it might hamper the production.
It includes the expenses of stores, equipment, personnel, insurance etc..
It locks up the capital of the enterprise.

qt: Which factor is often used to prioritize jobs when allocating resources?

While finding the Critical Path, in the phase of Early Start and Early Finish computations, we start from first event and proceed calculations from left to right up to the ---------- event.

In a network flow diagram, if two jobs ‘a(l,n)’ and ‘b(m,n)’ of ‘8’ and ‘9’ days durations respectively, start earlier simultaneously on 2nd day, then the next activity containing ‘n’ as head event can’t start until the entering activity_______________is completed.
About which of the following it is true that, “they only signify with the passage of time, the beginning and ending of some activities under no consumption of resources” ?
In the phase of Early Start and Early Finish, to find the Critical Path in a network flow diagram, for the first node(event), we start with time -------------.

Choose the correct one for the project times that are used in project management.
op: Latest start time, Latest finish time, Earliest start time, CPM
Latest start time, Latest finish time, PERT, Earliest finish time
Latest start time, Latest finish time, Earliest start time, Earliest finish time

op: doted line with left to right arrow
doted line with right to left arrow

qt: While finding the critical path in a network flow diagram, if an activity (i,j) starts and finish late on 3rd and 9th day respectively, then it will be completed in -----------days.
Which of the following phases are performed to find the critical path in CPM?
op: Forward and Backward(Early and Late finish program)

qt: Delay in critical activities for a project management lead to ________________.
op: project finish time will have to extend

qt: In a Transportation problem, which of the following are taken as the coefficients of unknown “\(x_{ij}\)'s” ?

If the total demand is equal to total supply as per requirement of a balanced transportation problem i-e “\(a_1+a_2+ \cdots +a_n = b_1+b_2+\cdots+b_n\)” then which of the following is true?
op: “\(a_1=b_1, a_2 =b_2,\cdots, a_n=b_n\)” is NOT necessarily implied

qt: Which values of “a_{ij}'s” in the standard form of a linear programming problem will transform it in standard Transportation problem?
op: a_{ij} = 0 or 1 for all i =1,2,\cdots,m; j = 1,2,\cdots,n
a_{ij} = 0 and 1 for all i =1,2,\cdots,m; j = 1,2,\cdots,n
